# Constructed via the aql:time module — the test runner pre-installs

# `reg compare/q` installs a Comparer on type T. The body receives the

# ordering: negative → a<b, zero → equal, positive → a>b.

# Body error propagates through the Comparer chain.
type Faulty object {x:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Faulty Faulty] [Integer] [quux]]) def v1 (make Faulty {x:1}) def v2 (make Faulty {x:2}) v1 v2 lt
# `reg canon/q` installs a Format-via-canon Behavior on T. The body
# receives the value as the def `a` and returns a String — that
# String becomes the value's canonical render (via Value.String,
# Render Person as just its name field. The body returns the raw
# string contents — that becomes the canonical render for Person
type Person object {name:String age:Integer} reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
# Multi-field canon body — string concat plus a fixed suffix.
type Person object {name:String age:Integer} reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get) ' (person)' add]]) make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}
# Canon coexists with compare on the same type — both wrap one
# userBehavior; installing the second does not lose the first.

type Person object {name:String age:Integer} reg compare/q (fn [[Person Person] [Integer] [(a 'age' get) (b 'age' get) sub]]) reg canon/q (fn [[Person] [String] [(a 'name' get)]]) def alice (make Person {name:'Alice' age:30}) def bob (make Person {name:'Bob' age:25}) alice bob lt
note: compare still works after canon installed
